source_pr.title: ddl: support altering the other charset to utf8 or utf8mb4
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? fix #7920. Support changing the other charset to utf8 or utf8mb4. Rules is: 1. change any other charset to utf8mb4 is permitted, because TiDB treat all data as utf8mb4 2. change any other charset to utf8 is permitted, except utf8mb4. Before this PR, `alter table charset` take no effects, we just parse it and ignore, in this PR, we change the charset and collate of tableInfo in the infoSchema. Tests - Unit test - Integration test Code changes - Has exported function/method change Side effects - Increased code complexity Related changes - Need to update the documentation - Need to be included in the release note --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/pingcap/tidb/8037)

symptoms: -// SetRowCount sets the number of rows. Make sure it can pass `make race`.
failure_signature: -// SetRowCount sets the number of rows. Make sure it can pass `make race`.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: ddl: support altering the other charset to utf8 or utf8mb4.
fix_pattern: ddl: support altering the other charset to utf8 or utf8mb4

evidence: evidence: -// SetRowCount sets the number of rows. Make sure it can pass `make race`.
